open_world_mechanisms: broad catalyst -> direct beneficiary check -> indirect capacity path
current evidence -> market narrative expansion -> leader selection uncertainty
first-order company event -> balance-sheet or contract relevance -> red-team review
blind_analysis.summary: Open-world blind analysis over current news, followed by memory and web verification hooks.

counterarguments: listing status or ticker may be unverified
news may not be economically attributable to the listed entity
disconfirming_conditions: event fully reflected before D-1 close
inferred_evidence: created by open-world pass before memory lookup
candidates.novel_reasoning: Candidate is not required to exist in memory before investigation.
candidates.thesis: Directly mentioned entity is a blind candidate pending listing, novelty, relation, and D-1 absorption checks.
candidates.why_now: It appears in the pre-cutoff news batch.

candidates.novel_reasoning: A new beneficiary can be investigated even when retrieval returns no cases.

market_memory_evidence: D-day prices are blocked during blind analysis
candidates.thesis: Recent leaders must be checked using only D-1 and earlier market data.
candidates.why_now: Continuation is evaluated separately from current-news directness.

dominant_sectors.expected_breadth: requires web verification and memory comparison
failure_conditions: web evidence fails listing or relation verification
D-1 market already absorbed the catalyst
